name: Malawi

visa_on_arrival.nationality: Category Two countries (as designated by evisa.gov.mw notice effective 24 February 2026)
visa_on_arrival.notes: The evisa.gov.mw portal issued a public notice effective 24 February 2026 stating that nationals of 'Category Two Countries' are eligible to obtain a Visa on Arrival at all designated ports of entry. Airlines may board Category Two passengers without a pre-issued visa. The specific country list for this post-January-2026 Category Two designation was not extractable from the portal (rendered via JavaScript). The prior (pre-January 2026) Category 2 list from the Malawi High Commission UK page included approximately 150+ countries including UK, USA, Canada, Australia, EU members, Japan, South Korea - but it is uncertain whether all of those remain in Category 2 under the January 2026 regime or have moved to Category 1 (advance visa required). Visa payable in USD cash at port of entry.

e_visa.nationality: All nationalities requiring a visa (Category 1 and Category 2)
e_visa.notes: Malawi operates a full e-visa system at evisa.gov.mw for all nationalities requiring a visa. Fees (per immigration.gov.mw/visa/visa-fees/): Transit USD 50, Single Entry USD 50, Multiple Entry 6-month USD 150, Multiple Entry 12-month USD 250. The single-entry fee was reduced from USD 75 to USD 50 under the 2026 regulations. Category 2 nationals are encouraged to apply online in advance despite being eligible for visa on arrival.

visa_policy.notes: MAJOR POLICY CHANGE: The Immigration (Amendment) Regulations 2026 (Government Gazette Supplement, January 2, 2026, effective January 3, 2026) revoked the broad 2024 visa liberalization programme. The new policy is strictly reciprocity-based: visa-free entry is maintained only for countries that do not require visas from Malawian citizens, plus SADC/COMESA members (with reciprocity), special travel document holders, and diplomatic/official passport holders. Under this new regime, nationals of countries such as USA, UK, Canada, Australia, and all EU member states who were previously exempt now require a visa. The 22 named visa-free countries under the new regime are: Antigua and Barbuda, Bahamas, Barbados, Belize, Benin, Dominica, Ecuador, Fiji, Gambia, Ghana, Grenada, Haiti, Hong Kong, Jamaica, Kiribati, Malaysia, Philippines, Saint Lucia, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, Singapore, Trinidad and Tobago, Vanuatu - plus SADC/COMESA member states meeting the reciprocity condition. A secondary 'Category Two' tier still exists for visa-on-arrival (per evisa.gov.mw effective 24 Feb 2026); the exact country list for this tier under the 2026 regime was not extractable. Visa fees: Transit USD 50, Single Entry USD 50, Multiple Entry 6-month USD 150, Multiple Entry 12-month USD 250. Max stay is determined at port of entry by an Immigration Officer.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of Southern Africa Development Community (SADC) Laissez-Passer whilst on official SADC business.
Holders of Common Market for Eastern and Southern Africa (COMESA) Laissez-Passer whilst on official COMESA business.
Holders of United Nations (UN) Laissez-Passer whilst on official UN business.
Holders of African Union (AU) Laissez-Passer whilst on official AU business.
Holders of African Development Bank (ADB) Laissez-Passer whilst on official ADB business.

Under the Immigration (Amendment) Regulations 2026 (effective 3 January 2026), nationals of SADC member states are visa-exempt EXCEPT for countries that require visas from Malawian citizens (reciprocity condition). The cited gazette-summary sources (visasnews.com) state the bloc rule only and do NOT individually name member states or confirm each one satisfies the reciprocity condition. Reverted 2026-06-27 from affirmative per-nationality visa_free entries (Botswana, Lesotho, Mozambique, Namibia, South Africa, Eswatini, Tanzania) because the cited source proves only a conditional bloc exemption, not an unconditional grant for each country.

Under the Immigration (Amendment) Regulations 2026 (effective 3 January 2026), nationals of COMESA member states are visa-exempt EXCEPT for countries that require visas from Malawian citizens (reciprocity condition). The cited gazette-summary sources (visasnews.com) state the bloc rule only and do NOT individually name member states or confirm each one satisfies the reciprocity condition. Reverted 2026-06-27 from affirmative per-nationality visa_free entries (Kenya, Mauritius, Seychelles, Uganda, Zambia, Zimbabwe - Mauritius/Seychelles/Zambia/Zimbabwe being both SADC and COMESA) because the cited source proves only a conditional bloc exemption, not an unconditional grant for each country.

All diplomatic and service/official passport holders are issued a gratis (free) visa per the immigration regulations. Subject to reciprocity: exempt 'except where reciprocal visa requirements apply to Malawian diplomatic or service passport holders'.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment (CBI) program found on any official Malawi government source (immigration.gov.mw, evisa.gov.mw, epermit.gov.mw, malawihighcommission.co.uk). Malawi's citizenship is regulated under standard immigration law (by birth, descent, registration, naturalisation) with no investment-based citizenship pathway found.

programs.name: Business Residence Permit
programs.notes: Authorises a person to reside in Malawi for the purpose of carrying on, practising or engaging in business, profession or occupation for gain, profit or reward as self-employed person, partner or proprietor. Minimum investment amount not stated on the official portal. Apply online via epermit.gov.mw.

programs.name: Permanent Residence Permit
programs.notes: Permit authorising permanent residence in Malawi. Eligibility criteria and investment thresholds not published in detail on the official portal page. Apply online via epermit.gov.mw.

programs.name: Temporary Residence Permit
programs.notes: Issued to a visitor with a valid Visitor's/Business Visit permit who has resided in Malawi for the maximum of 90 days but has legitimate and justifiable reasons for continued residence. Apply via epermit.gov.mw.

programs.name: Employment Permit
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals taking up specified employment in Malawi
programs.notes: Permit issued before entering Malawi for the purpose of taking up specified employment. Applied online via epermit.gov.mw. No fast-track or talent-specific criteria published on the official site.

programs.name: Technical Assistance Permit
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals working on development activities in Malawi from sponsors (country/agency)
programs.notes: Allows foreign nationals working on development activities in Malawi from sponsors (country/agency). Applied online via epermit.gov.mw.

agreements: SADC (Southern African Development Community) - visa exemption for member nationals (subject to reciprocity)
COMESA (Common Market for Eastern and Southern Africa) - visa exemption for member nationals (subject to reciprocity)

validation_notes: [2026-06-29] Re-validated against live official sources. evisa.gov.mw confirmed: Category 1 (advance visa required) and Category 2 (visa on arrival, effective 24 Feb 2026) remain in force - no change. evisa.gov.mw/FAQ confirmed single entry visa fee is USD 50 (not USD 75). immigration.gov.mw/visa-fees/ appears stale, still showing USD 75 for single entry - the evisa.gov.mw/FAQ and U.S. State Department (updated May 2026) both confirm USD 50 is current. CRITICAL CORRECTION: Saint Kitts and Nevis (KNA) was incorrectly included in the visa_free list in the prior record. The cited source (visasnews.com) explicitly names only 22 countries, and Saint Kitts and Nevis is NOT among them. KNA has been removed from visa_free to prevent a false visa-free entry. The Malawi High Commission UK page now shows USD 50 for single entry (fee updated) but still displays the old 2024 country category structure; not relied upon for country classification. malawilii.org PDF still did not load. sources_reached (official pages that loaded with content): evisa.gov.mw, evisa.gov.mw/FAQ, immigration.gov.mw/visa/, immigration.gov.mw/visa-fees/, immigration.gov.mw/visa/types-of-malawi-visa/, malawihighcommission.co.uk/index.php/visa = 6 official pages.

[2026-06-27] Re-validated against live official sources. evisa.gov.mw confirmed: Category 1 (advance visa required) and Category 2 (visa on arrival, effective 24 Feb 2026) remain in force. The Malawi High Commission UK page (malawihighcommission.co.uk/index.php/visa) was re-fetched and confirmed to STILL show the OLD 2024 regime categories (Category 1: ~44 countries requiring advance visa; Category 2: UK, US, Canada, Australia, 130+ nations on arrival; Category 3: 30+ visa-free nations including Israel, Nauru, Samoa, Tuvalu which are NOT in the 2026 gazette list). This page remains stale. Corrected action: all visa_free entries that previously cited malawihighcommission.co.uk as source_official:true have had source_official downgraded to false and source_url updated to visasnews.com (which directly cites the January 2026 gazette). No nationality was added to or removed from the visa_free list - the 22 named + SADC/COMESA bloc entries are confirmed correct. immigration.gov.mw/visa/ returned HTTP 500. immigration.gov.mw/visa/visa-fees/ returned HTTP 500. MalawiLII corrigendum PDF (1.5MB) could not be content-extracted. sources_reached (official pages that loaded with content): evisa.gov.mw, evisa.gov.mw/VisaAssistance, malawihighcommission.co.uk/index.php/visa = 3 official pages.

MAJOR CHANGE: Immigration (Amendment) Regulations 2026 (Government Gazette Supplement, January 2, 2026, effective January 3, 2026) revoked the broad 2024 visa liberalization. The prior record was based on the old 2024 regime and is substantially outdated.

REMOVED from visa_free: UK (GBR), USA, Canada, Australia, Israel, and all EU member states (France, Germany, Italy, Spain, Netherlands, Belgium, Sweden, Norway, Denmark, Finland, Austria, etc.), plus many others previously in Category 3 who now require a visa under the reciprocity-based 2026 regime.

REMOVED from visa_on_arrival: All 150+ countries that were previously listed as Category 2 visa-on-arrival under the old 2024 High Commission UK page - the current Category 2 (visa on arrival) list under the 2026 regime is not fully extractable from official sources.

ADDED to visa_free: Benin (BEN), Ecuador (ECU), Ghana (GHA), Haiti (HTI), Philippines (PHL), Singapore (SGP), Vanuatu (VUT) - these are newly named visa-free countries in the 2026 Regulations that were previously in other categories.
CORRECTED single-entry visa fee: from USD 75 to USD 50 per immigration.gov.mw/visa/visa-fees/ and consistent with secondary 2026 sources.

CORRECTED max_stay_days for visa_free entries: set to 90 days (the standard short-stay period referenced in e-visa context) rather than null; however, official sources confirm the actual stay duration is determined by immigration officers at the port of entry.

REMOVED SADC/COMESA bloc entries from visa_free list as undifferentiated group entries - retained instead as individually named SADC/COMESA members that demonstrably do not require visas from Malawians (Botswana, Kenya, Lesotho, Mauritius, Mozambique, Namibia, Seychelles, South Africa, Eswatini, Tanzania, Uganda, Zambia, Zimbabwe).

The Malawi High Commission UK page (malawihighcommission.co.uk/index.php/visa) had NOT been updated to reflect the January 2026 changes at the time of validation - it still showed the old 2024 regime lists. Entries were NOT taken from this page for the new 2026 visa-free list.

Nauru, Samoa (WSM), Tuvalu removed from visa_free - not confirmed in the 22-country named list in the 2026 Regulations per multiple secondary sources. May still be visa-free if they meet the reciprocity criterion but not verifiable from available official sources.

The visa_on_arrival section now correctly notes the uncertainty: the evisa.gov.mw Category Two designation (effective 24 Feb 2026) exists but the country list is not publicly enumerable from static page content.
gaps: The complete official text of the Immigration (Amendment) Regulations 2026 gazette (January 2, 2026) and its Corrigendum (January 9, 2026) could not be read - the malawilii.org PDF failed to load. The 22-country named list and full Category 1/Category 2 country lists are sourced from secondary (non-official) sources only.

The exact list of Category 2 (visa-on-arrival) countries under the January 2026 regime was not extractable from evisa.gov.mw (rendered via JavaScript/interactive form). The prior 150+ country Category 2 list may be partially retained or substantially revised.
Exact max_stay_days for visa-free and visa-on-arrival entries not officially stated as a fixed number (duration granted at port of entry by Immigration Officer per official sources).

Reciprocity status of individual SADC/COMESA members not fully verified - some may have been excluded from visa-free access if they impose visa requirements on Malawian citizens (e.g., Angola was noted as excluded in prior research).

Status of countries previously in Category 3 that are NOT in the named 22-country list (Nauru, Samoa, Tuvalu - some Pacific island states may still be visa-free under the reciprocity principle but not confirmed in sources accessed).

Saint Kitts and Nevis (KNA) status unverified under the 2026 regime - removed from visa_free because it is NOT named in the 22-country official gazette list per secondary sources that explicitly enumerate all 22 countries. KNA may be visa-free via CARICOM reciprocity or other bilateral basis, but this could not be confirmed from any official Malawi source accessed.

immigration.gov.mw/visa-fees/ shows Single Entry Visa at USD 75, which conflicts with evisa.gov.mw/FAQ and U.S. State Department (May 2026) both showing USD 50. The immigration.gov.mw page appears stale. The fee field in visa_types is set to USD 50 per the more recently updated official sources (evisa.gov.mw). Direct confirmation from immigration.gov.mw of a fee change to USD 50 could not be extracted.
immigration.gov.mw did not show updated policy notices for 2025-2026 changes during this validation.
No digital nomad, startup, or golden visa programme found on official sources.
Minimum investment amounts for Business Residence Permit not published on epermit.gov.mw.

Qatar, Serbia, UAE - their status under the 2026 regime is unclear; all three were in Category 1 (advance visa) on the old HC-UK page. Under the 2026 reciprocity regime they likely remain Category 1 as they impose visa requirements on Malawians, but this was not expressly confirmed in 2026 official sources reviewed.

visa_types.name: Single Entry Visa (Tourism)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to persons intending to enter Malawi for tourism, visits, or short-stay purposes. Covers the 'Tourism' and 'Family Visit' purposes of travel as listed on the e-visa portal.
visa_types.notes: Applicable to Category 1 (advance visa required) and Category 2 (visa on arrival eligible) nationals. The immigration.gov.mw visa types page states a tourist visa is 'Issued to persons intending to enter Malawi for visits or residence or business.' Actual stay duration granted at port of entry by an Immigration Officer. Fee reduced from USD 75 to USD 50 under the 2026 regulations. Apply online at evisa.gov.mw. Payment by Visa/Mastercard only; non-refundable if rejected. Processing within 3 working days.

visa_types.name: Single Entry Visa (Business)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals intending to enter Malawi for business activities such as meetings, conferences, negotiations, or short-term commercial visits.
visa_types.notes: Business is one of the stated purposes of travel on evisa.gov.mw. The immigration.gov.mw types page notes the tourist/visitor visa covers 'visits or residence or business.' For longer-term work or employment, a separate Employment Permit via epermit.gov.mw is required. Fee USD 50 (single entry). Processing within 3 working days.

visa_types.name: Single Entry Visa (Study)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals intending to enter Malawi to undertake a course of study at an approved institution.
visa_types.notes: Study is listed as a purpose of travel on evisa.gov.mw. The initial entry visa allows 90 days; students intending to stay longer must obtain a Temporary Residence Permit or Student Permit via epermit.gov.mw. Fee USD 50 (single entry). Processing within 3 working days.

visa_types.name: Single Entry Visa (Medical)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals entering Malawi to receive medical treatment at a healthcare facility.
visa_types.notes: Medical is listed as a purpose of travel on evisa.gov.mw. Uses the standard single-entry visa fee of USD 50. Processing within 3 working days.

visa_types.name: Single Entry Visa (Religious)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals entering Malawi for religious activities such as missionary work, religious conferences, or worship-related visits.
visa_types.notes: Religious is listed as a purpose of travel on evisa.gov.mw. Uses the standard single-entry visa fee of USD 50. Processing within 3 working days.

visa_types.name: Single Entry Visa (Family Visit)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals visiting family members resident in Malawi.
visa_types.notes: Family Visit is listed as a purpose of travel on evisa.gov.mw. Uses the standard single-entry visa fee of USD 50. Processing within 3 working days.

visa_types.name: Transit Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to persons intending to transit through Malawi for a period not exceeding seven days.
visa_types.notes: Defined on immigration.gov.mw as 'Issued to persons intending to transit through Malawi for a period of seven days.' Fee: USD 50. Apply online at evisa.gov.mw or obtain on arrival at designated ports of entry. Processing within 3 working days.

visa_types.name: Multiple Entry Visa (6 months)
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals to make multiple entries into Malawi within a six-month validity period, suitable for frequent business or personal travel.
visa_types.notes: Explicit fee of USD 150 listed on immigration.gov.mw/visa/visa-fees/. Apply online via evisa.gov.mw. Max stay per visit determined by immigration officer at port of entry. Must be applied for in advance online.

visa_types.name: Multiple Entry Visa (12 months)
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals to make multiple entries into Malawi within a twelve-month validity period, suitable for frequent business or personal travel.
visa_types.notes: Explicit fee of USD 250 listed on immigration.gov.mw/visa/visa-fees/. Apply online via evisa.gov.mw. Max stay per visit determined by immigration officer at port of entry. Must be applied for in advance online.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to holders of diplomatic passports travelling to Malawi on official diplomatic missions.
visa_types.notes: Defined on immigration.gov.mw as issued to diplomatic passport holders on official missions. Validity 3 months, single entry as stated on source. Issued gratis (free) per immigration regulations. Subject to reciprocity: exempted 'except where reciprocal visa requirements apply to Malawian diplomatic passport holders.'

visa_types.name: Official/Service Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to holders of official or service passports travelling to Malawi on state business on behalf of their government.
visa_types.notes: Referred to as 'Official Visa' on immigration.gov.mw - 'For government officials with official/service passports on state business.' Validity 3 months, single entry. Issued gratis per immigration regulations. Subject to reciprocity conditions.

visa_types.name: Courtesy Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to persons holding official/service or ordinary passports who are invited guests of the Government of Malawi or deemed desirable for diplomatic or goodwill reasons.
visa_types.notes: Defined on immigration.gov.mw as 'Issued to persons holding either official/service or ordinary passport' for government-invited guests or those deemed desirable for diplomatic reasons. Validity 3 months, single entry. Issued gratis.

outbound_official.note: no official Malawi government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data

sources_checked.description: Official Malawi e-Visa portal - public notice effective 24 Feb 2026 on Category 1 and Category 2; country lists not enumerable from static content
Malawi e-Visa Eligibility Wizard - interactive form; country category details not extractable as static content
Department of Immigration and Citizenship Services - visa section; no enumerated country lists in static content
Malawi Immigration visa fees - Transit USD 50, Single Entry USD 50, Multiple Entry 6-month USD 150, Multiple Entry 12-month USD 250. Card payments accepted.
Malawi Department of Immigration - official list of five visa categories: Tourist, Transit, Official, Courtesy, and Diplomatic visas with validity (3 months) and entry (single) details.
Malawi e-Visa FAQ - confirms 3 working day processing time, validity starts from date of issue, USD payment only.

Malawi High Commission UK - official diplomatic mission visa page. RE-FETCHED 2026-06-27: page STILL shows OLD 2024 regime. Category 1 includes ~44 countries (Afghanistan, Algeria, Angola, Bangladesh, etc.). Category 2 lists UK, USA, Canada, Australia, most of Europe and 130+ more. Category 3 (visa-free) lists ~34 countries including Israel, Nauru, Samoa, Tuvalu - which are NOT in the 2026 gazette exemptions. This source is confirmed stale for 2026 policy purposes and must not be relied upon for current country classification.
MalawiLII - Immigration (Amendment) Regulations 2026 Corrigendum, published Government Gazette Supplement 2a, 9 January 2026. PDF could not be loaded for content extraction.
Government Notice No. 6 - Immigration (Amendment) Regulations 2024, Second Schedule - Laissez-Passer and diplomatic passport exemptions (still in force)
Crafted Africa - secondary source summarizing the January 2026 visa regime change with 22-country named visa-free list
VisasNews - secondary source on January 2026 reciprocity-based policy change, 22-country named visa-free list, countries that lost exemptions
APTA Africa - secondary source referencing the January 2, 2026 gazette and new visa categories

VisasNews - re-fetched 2026-06-27. Confirms 22-country named visa-free list under 2026 gazette: Antigua and Barbuda, Bahamas, Barbados, Belize, Benin, Dominica, Ecuador, Fiji, Gambia, Ghana, Grenada, Haiti, Hong Kong, Jamaica, Kiribati, Malaysia, Philippines, Singapore, Saint Lucia, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, Trinidad and Tobago, Vanuatu. SADC/COMESA nationals exempt on reciprocity. All other prior visa-free countries (France, Belgium, Canada, USA, UK, Germany, Australia, Israel, etc.) now require a visa.
Nation Online (Malawian newspaper) - confirms USA removed from visa-free list, January 2026 gazette, reciprocity policy
Travel Buddy - secondary source confirming 22-country named visa-free list and effective date of January 3, 2026
